# azuredevops_group (Resource)

Manages a group within Azure DevOps.

## Example Usage

### Basic

```terraform
resource "azuredevops_group" "test" {
  display_name = "example-group"
}
```

### With Members

```terraform
resource "azuredevops_group" "test" {
  display_name = "example-group"
  description  = "description"
  members = [
    azuredevops_group.member1.id,
    azuredevops_group.member2.id,
  ]
}

resource "azuredevops_group" "member1" {
  display_name = "example-member1"
}
resource "azuredevops_group" "member2" {
  display_name = "example-member2"
}
```

### Project Scope

```terraform
resource "azuredevops_project" "test" {
  name = "example-project"
}

resource "azuredevops_group" "test" {
  scope        = azuredevops_project.test.id
  display_name = "example-group"
}
```

### Created from AAD Group Id

```terraform
data "azuread_client_config" "current" {}

resource "azuread_group" "test" {
  display_name     = "example-group"
  owners           = [data.azuread_client_config.current.object_id]
  security_enabled = true
}

resource "azuredevops_group" "test" {
  origin_id = azuread_group.test.object_id
}
```

### Created from AAD mail

```terraform
data "azuread_client_config" "current" {}

resource "azuread_group" "test" {
  display_name     = "example-group"
  mail_enabled     = true
  mail_nickname    = "example-mail"
  types            = ["Unified"]
  owners           = [data.azuread_client_config.current.object_id]
  security_enabled = true
}

resource "azuredevops_group" "test" {
  mail = azuread_group.test.mail
}
```

## Schema

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the group. Value defaults to ``.

	-> Ensure that if an attribute is set, these are not set: "[origin_id,mail]".
- `display_name` (String) The display name of the VSTS group.

	-> String length must be at least 1.

	-> Ensure that one and only one attribute from this collection is set: "[display_name,mail,origin_id]".
- `mail` (String) This will create a new graph group that is derived from the email of an AAD group.

	~> If the value of this attribute changes, Terraform will destroy and recreate the resource.

	~> Once set, the value of this attribute in state will not change.

	-> String length must be at least 1.

	-> Ensure that one and only one attribute from this collection is set: "[display_name,mail,origin_id]".
- `members` (Set of Strings) A set of member descriptors. Value defaults to `[]`.
- `origin_id` (String) This will create a new graph group that is derived from the object id of an AAD group.

	~> If the value of this attribute changes, Terraform will destroy and recreate the resource.

	~> Once set, the value of this attribute in state will not change.

	-> String must be in UUID format.

	-> Ensure that one and only one attribute from this collection is set: "[display_name,mail,origin_id]".
- `scope` (String) The id of the scope (e.g. project) in which the group should be created. If omitted, will be created in the scope of the enclosing account or organization. Valid only for VSTS groups.

	~> If the value of this attribute changes, Terraform will destroy and recreate the resource.

	~> Once set, the value of this attribute in state will not change.

	-> String must be in UUID format.

	-> Ensure that if an attribute is set, these are not set: "[origin_id,mail]".
- `timeouts` (Object) See [below for nested schema](#nested--timeouts).

### Read-Only

- `domain` (String) The name of the container fof origin for the group.
- `id` (String) The descriptor of the group

	~> Once set, the value of this attribute in state will not change.
- `origin` (String) The type of source provider for the group.
- `principal_name` (String) The principal name of this group from the source provider.
- `storage_key` (String) The storage key of the group's descriptor.
- `subject_kind` (String) The type of the graph subject for the group.
- `url` (String) The full route to the group.

<a id="nested--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) (Defaults to 5m0s) Used when creating this resource.

	-> Must be a string containing a sequence of decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix, such as "300ms", "-1.5h" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".
- `delete` (String) (Defaults to 5m0s) Used when deleting this resource.

	-> Must be a string containing a sequence of decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix, such as "300ms", "-1.5h" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".
- `read` (String) (Defaults to 5m0s) Used when reading this resource.

	-> Must be a string containing a sequence of decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix, such as "300ms", "-1.5h" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".
- `update` (String) (Defaults to 5m0s) Used when updating this resource.

	-> Must be a string containing a sequence of decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix, such as "300ms", "-1.5h" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".

## Import

### Import ID

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used with the id format:

```shell
<group_id>
```

For example:

```shell
$ terraform import azuredevops_group.example "vssgp.xxxx"
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = azuredevops_group.example
  id = "vssgp.xxxx"
}
```

### Import Identity

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute.

```terraform
import {
  to = azuredevops_group.example
  identity = {
    id = "vssgp.xxxx"
  }
}
```

Required:

- `id` (String) The descriptor of the group
